title: Europe Faces Dangerous Heatwave as Extreme Temperatures Move East
subtitle: As western nations begin to recover, central and eastern Europe face record-breaking heat and public health challenges.

summary.en: A severe heatwave is moving across Europe, bringing record-breaking temperatures to central and eastern countries. Authorities have issued red alerts as cities like Budapest expect to reach 40 degrees Celsius. Meanwhile, France is dealing with the tragic aftermath of the heat, which caused over 1,000 excess deaths. Funeral directors in Paris report that mortuaries are completely full due to the high number of fatalities. Scientists warn that this extreme weather is a direct result of the ongoing climate crisis.
summary.zh: 一波嚴重的熱浪正橫掃歐洲，為中東部國家帶來破紀錄的高溫。當局已發布紅色警戒，布達佩斯等城市預計氣溫將達到攝氏四十度。同時，法國正處理高溫帶來的悲劇後果，已造成超過一千例超額死亡。巴黎的殯葬業者表示，由於死亡人數眾多，殯儀館已完全客滿。科學家警告，這種極端天氣是持續中的氣候危機所導致的直接結果。

paragraphs.en: A dangerous heatwave is currently moving across Europe, shifting from the west toward central and eastern regions. After breaking records in western countries, the extreme heat is now creating serious risks for millions of people in places like Hungary, Serbia, and Romania. According to The Guardian, the "heat dome"—a weather pattern that traps hot air over a large area—is responsible for these life-threatening conditions.
paragraphs.zh: 一波危險的熱浪目前正橫掃歐洲，從西部向中東部地區移動。在打破西歐國家的氣溫紀錄後，這波極端高溫現在正對匈牙利、塞爾維亞和羅馬尼亞等地的數百萬人造成嚴重威脅。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，這種將熱空氣困在廣大區域的氣象型態，被稱為「熱穹」，正是導致這些危及生命狀況的元兇。

paragraphs.en: On Tuesday, Budapest is expected to see temperatures climb above 40 degrees Celsius. Other cities are already feeling the impact; Belgrade and Bucharest recently recorded temperatures of 38 and 37 degrees Celsius. In Slovakia, a new national record was set when a town near the border reached 40.5 degrees Celsius, beating a record that had stood since 2007. Governments across the region have issued red alerts, which are the highest level of warning for extreme weather. Authorities are strongly advising people to stay indoors during the hottest parts of the day to avoid heatstroke and other health problems.
paragraphs.zh: 週二，布達佩斯預計氣溫將攀升至攝氏四十度以上。其他城市也已感受到衝擊；貝爾格勒和布加勒斯特近期記錄到的氣溫分別為攝氏三十八度和三十七度。在斯洛伐克，一個靠近邊境的城鎮測得攝氏四十點五度，打破了自二〇〇七年以來維持至今的紀錄，創下全國新高。該地區各國政府已發布紅色警戒，這是極端天氣的最高等級警告。當局強烈建議民眾在一天中最炎熱的時段待在室內，以避免中暑及其他健康問題。

paragraphs.en: In response to the crisis, Hungarian officials have opened more than 2,000 air-conditioned cooling centers for those who do not have a way to stay cool at home. Hungary’s prime minister, Péter Magyar, used social media to call for national unity, asking citizens to look out for one another during these difficult days. Meanwhile, the country’s energy minister has allowed the Paks nuclear power plant to use warmer water for cooling. This temporary rule change is necessary to keep the plant running and prevent power outages during the heatwave.
paragraphs.zh: 為了應對這場危機，匈牙利官員已開放超過兩千個裝有空調的避暑中心，供家中無法降溫的民眾使用。匈牙利總理馬札爾（Péter Magyar）透過社群媒體呼籲全國團結，請求公民在這段艱難的日子裡互相照應。同時，該國能源部長已准許帕克斯（Paks）核電廠使用較溫暖的水進行冷卻。這項臨時性的規則變更對於維持電廠運作、防止熱浪期間發生停電至關重要。

paragraphs.en: While the heat is now moving east, the situation remains dire in western Europe, where the worst of the weather has begun to fade. France has reported more than 1,000 excess deaths—a term used to describe deaths that are higher than the usual number for that time of year—linked to the extreme temperatures. According to MedPage Today, the public health agency in France noted that the death rate spiked significantly during the hottest days of last week. Many of those who died were people aged 65 and older, and there was a 40 percent increase in deaths occurring at home.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管熱浪正向東移動，西歐的情況依然嚴峻，儘管當地最惡劣的天氣已開始減弱。法國通報了超過一千例的超額死亡——這是一個用來描述死亡人數高於往年同期平均值的術語——這些死亡皆與極端高溫有關。根據《醫學頁面今日》（MedPage Today）報導，法國公共衛生機構指出，死亡率在上週最炎熱的幾天內顯著飆升。死者多為六十五歲以上的長者，且發生在家中的死亡案例增加了百分之四十。

paragraphs.en: The impact on public services has been severe. In Paris, funeral directors are struggling to manage the high number of deaths. Zouhaier Hertelli, a mortuary owner, told MedPage Today that his facility is completely full. "We're facing a really catastrophic situation," he said, explaining that he receives hundreds of calls from families seeking help. Because local storage is full, some funeral directors have been forced to move bodies to facilities as far as 50 miles away from the capital. City officials have responded by installing temporary refrigerated units to provide more space.
paragraphs.zh: 公共服務受到的影響相當嚴重。在巴黎，殯葬業者正疲於應付大量的死亡案例。殯儀館負責人赫泰利（Zouhaier Hertelli）向《醫學頁面今日》表示，他的設施已經完全客滿。他解釋自己接到數百通家屬尋求協助的電話，並表示：「我們正面臨一場真正的災難。」由於當地存放空間已滿，一些殯葬業者被迫將遺體移送到距離首都遠達五十英里（約八十公里）的設施。市政府官員已採取行動，安裝臨時冷藏櫃以提供更多空間。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the human cost, the heat is causing widespread disruption. In Germany, high temperatures caused tram tracks to bend, forcing changes to public transport. In some cities, police even used water cannons to help cool down crowds of people in public spaces. In Croatia, firefighters have been working to control a wildfire on the island of Vis, while in Italy, the extreme weather has taken a different form. Torrential rain and storms in the Alto Adige region led to flash floods and landslides, forcing some residents to leave their homes.
paragraphs.zh: 除了人命損失，高溫也造成了廣泛的混亂。在德國，高溫導致電車軌道彎曲，迫使大眾運輸必須調整。在某些城市，警方甚至使用水砲來協助公共空間的民眾降溫。在克羅埃西亞，消防人員正努力控制維斯島（Vis）上的野火；而在義大利，極端天氣則以不同形式呈現。上阿迪傑（Alto Adige）地區的豪雨與風暴引發了山洪與土石流，迫使部分居民撤離家園。

paragraphs.en: For many, the situation is a clear sign that the climate is changing. Scientists have stated that this heatwave, which is described as the most severe and widespread in history, is only possible because of the climate crisis caused by burning fossil fuels. Residents are feeling the pressure to see change. Susanne, a resident of Vienna, told The Guardian that she spends her days trying to stay in the shade and drinking plenty of water. She expressed a hope that politicians will finally understand the urgency of the situation and take action to protect the environment.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多人來說，這種情況清楚顯示氣候正在改變。科學家指出，這波被形容為史上最嚴重且範圍最廣的熱浪，唯有在燃燒化石燃料所引發的氣候危機下才可能發生。居民們正感受到要求改變的壓力。維也納居民蘇珊（Susanne）告訴《衛報》，她整天都試著待在陰涼處並大量飲水。她表達了希望政治人物能最終理解局勢的急迫性，並採取行動保護環境。

paragraphs.en: As the heatwave continues to affect new parts of the continent, the focus remains on protecting vulnerable populations. With the death toll expected to rise as more data is collected, the coming days will be a test of how well European nations can manage the health and safety of their citizens during this record-breaking summer.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著熱浪持續影響歐洲大陸的新區域，焦點仍放在保護弱勢族群上。隨著更多數據收集完成，預計死亡人數將會上升，接下來的幾天將考驗歐洲各國在今年這個破紀錄的夏天，能多妥善地管理公民的健康與安全。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have remained valid or unchanged for a period of time.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「站立」，這裡指紀錄維持了某段時間。文中：beating a record that had stood since 2007.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To take care of someone or ensure they are safe.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 照顧、照料、留心某人的安全。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思容易聯想成「向外看」，這裡指互相照顧。文中：asking citizens to look out for one another during these difficult days.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To rise sharply or reach a high level suddenly.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（尖刺），這裡作動詞形容數據突然飆升。文中：the death rate spiked significantly during the hottest days of last week.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To assume a specific form or character.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「拿、取」，這裡指天氣呈現出不同的型態。文中：in Italy, the extreme weather has taken a different form.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Dangerous temperatures forecast for parts of Europe as heatwave moves east

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: Europe's Record Heat Has Overwhelmed Paris Mortuaries and Left Families in Distress

previousArticles.title: Europe Struggles as Record-Breaking Heatwave Moves East
previousArticles.summaryEn: Europe is experiencing a dangerous heatwave that has set new temperature records in several countries. Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic have all recorded temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius. Health officials report over 1,300 excess deaths linked to the extreme heat. Authorities are urging residents to stay hydrated and avoid strenuous activity. Climate change is being cited as a primary driver for these increasingly frequent and severe weather events.

previousArticles.title: Europe Struggles as Record-Breaking Heatwave Grips the Continent
previousArticles.summaryEn: A severe heatwave is causing record-breaking temperatures across Europe, leading to dangerous conditions for millions. Countries including the UK, France, and Germany have seen their highest temperatures in decades. Authorities have issued red alerts and taken emergency measures to protect public health. Economists warn that the extreme heat is threatening productivity and damaging economic growth. Experts suggest that Europe must adapt its infrastructure to handle these increasingly common weather events.

previousArticles.title: Europe Faces Record-Breaking Heatwave as Temperatures Soar
previousArticles.summaryEn: Europe is currently experiencing an intense and dangerous heatwave that has set new temperature records in several countries. Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic have all reported temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius. Health officials are concerned about the rising number of deaths, particularly among older people. Authorities have issued warnings and taken emergency measures to help residents stay cool. Experts link this extreme weather to the ongoing effects of climate change.

quiz.question: What specific measure did Hungarian authorities take to ensure the stability of the power grid during the heatwave?

options.A: They closed the Paks nuclear power plant to prevent overheating.
options.B: They allowed the Paks nuclear power plant to use warmer water for cooling.
options.C: They increased the number of air-conditioned cooling centers for citizens.
options.D: They implemented a national ban on using air conditioning during the day.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，匈牙利能源部長為了防止停電，允許 Paks 核電廠使用較溫暖的水進行冷卻，這是一項臨時的規則變更。選項 A 與文章所述相反，電廠並未關閉；選項 C 雖然文章提到有開設冷卻中心，但這與維持電網穩定無直接關係；選項 D 文章並未提及有此禁令。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what can be inferred about the impact of the heatwave on the elderly in France?

options.A: They were the only demographic group affected by the record-breaking temperatures.
options.B: They were more likely to die at home rather than in hospitals or public spaces.
options.C: They were the primary reason why funeral directors in Paris had to move bodies 50 miles away.
options.D: They were the main group responsible for the 40 percent increase in deaths occurring in hospitals.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段，法國報告了超過 1,000 例超額死亡，其中許多死者為 65 歲以上長者，且家中死亡人數增加了 40%，推論出長者在極端高溫下在家中發生意外的風險極高。選項 A 過度推論，文中未說只有長者受影響；選項 C 雖然提到喪葬壓力，但未明確指出僅因長者導致；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到的是「在家中」死亡率增加，而非醫院。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'catastrophic' mean as used in the context of the funeral director's statement?

options.A: A situation that is slightly inconvenient.
options.B: A state of being extremely organized.
options.C: A disastrous or extremely unfortunate event.
options.D: A process that happens very slowly over time.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文中提到殯儀館館長面對大量死亡人數，設施已滿，必須將遺體運送到 50 英里外，這顯示情況非常嚴重且具毀滅性。故 catastrophic 在此意指「災難性的」。選項 A 與文意嚴重不符；選項 B 與災難定義相反；選項 D 錯誤，這是一場突發且急迫的危機，並非緩慢的過程。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of the article regarding the current European heatwave?

options.A: To provide a scientific explanation of how heat domes form.
options.B: To compare the climate policies of France, Hungary, and Germany.
options.C: To describe the wide-ranging health, social, and environmental impacts of the extreme heat.
options.D: To argue that the heatwave is a temporary weather event that will soon disappear.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文全面探討了熱浪對歐洲多國造成的影響，包括健康威脅（死亡率）、基礎設施損壞（鐵軌彎曲、停電風險）、社會應對（冷卻中心、殯葬壓力）以及氣候變遷的關聯。選項 A 僅是開頭提到的背景；選項 B 並未深入比較政策，僅提及各國應對措施；選項 D 與文意不符，文章強調這是氣候危機的警訊，而非單純的短期天氣現象。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
